title: MathDial: Math Tutoring Dialogue Dataset
description: MathDial is a dataset of 2,861 one-to-one math tutoring dialogues grounded in GSM8K word problems, annotated with a four-category teacher-move taxonomy. This Potato config reproduces those teacher-move labels.
overview: MathDial is a dialogue tutoring dataset introduced by Macina, Daheim, Chowdhury, Sinha, Kapur, Gurevych, and Sachan from ETH Zurich and TU Darmstadt, published in Findings of the Association for Computational Linguistics: ACL 2023.

The dataset contains 2,861 one-to-one teacher-student tutoring dialogues. Each conversation is grounded in a multi-step math word problem drawn from GSM8K and a specific student confusion, with 91 teachers role-playing the tutor while a language model simulated the student.

Teacher utterances are labeled with a taxonomy of four pedagogical moves: Focus, Probing, Telling, and Generic. The taxonomy supports research on scaffolding strategies and evaluation of AI-generated tutoring responses for grade-school math.

The Potato config below reproduces the teacher-move annotation task: annotators read a tutoring turn, classify it under the four-category taxonomy, and mark the student confusion and key concepts in the dialogue.
citation:
